title: Citi sees little sign of overheating in Japan’s high-tech sector
body: Oops, something went wrong Investing.com -- Japanese equities have staged a sharp recovery in April, with the Nikkei 225 breaking above 60,000 and hitting year-to-date highs, but Citi says investors need not fear a repeat of last year's tech-driven correction. The rally has been heavily concentrated in AI and semiconductor-related stocks, pushing the Nikkei/TOPIX ratio to an all-time high of 16.2. Citi noted that "the rise in the N/T ratio in April can be almost entirely attributed to outperformance in the electric appliances and information and communications sectors," which carry high Nikkei 225 weightings. A similar dynamic is said to have played out last September and October, when high-tech stocks subsequently underperformed from November as the Nikkei corrected. This time, however,

title: Nikkei Rises 1.4%, Closing Above 60000 for First Time
body: CONCLUDED Last Updated: 15 hours ago By Kosaku Narioka, Dow Jones Newswires Japan's Nikkei Stock Average climbed 1.4% to 60537.36, closing above the 60000 threshold for the first time. Electronics stocks led the gains, with both Fanuc and Keyence jumping 16% following their quarterly results. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield rose 3 basis points to 2.465%. The dollar was at 159.38 yen, little changed from Y159.37 late Friday in New York.
